findings.evidence: `rg` hits: lines 55, 97, 117, and 173 all have `@[expose]`; the rubric allows this only when consumers must unfold or compute.
findings.fix: Remove the `@[expose]` attributes and keep the API usable via explicit `[simp]`/characteristic lemmas instead of cross-module unfolding.
findings.issue: Several public definitions expose their bodies (`normalizerQuotientMk`, `toNormalizerOfNormal`, `normalizerQuotientToQuotientOfNormal`, `normalizerQuotientEquivQuotientOfNormal`) even though representative lemmas are already provided or should be added.

findings.evidence: Line 182 gives only `normalizerQuotientEquivQuotientOfNormal_mk`; `rg -n "symm.*normalizerQuotientEquivQuotientOfNormal|normalizerQuotientEquivQuotientOfNormal.*symm"` finds no inverse representative lemma.
findings.fix: Add a `[simp]` lemma such as `(normalizerQuotientEquivQuotientOfNormal H).symm (QuotientGroup.mk' H g) = normalizerQuotientMk H (toNormalizerOfNormal H g)`, and analogous inverse/comp lemmas if needed by the target.
findings.issue: The normal-case equivalence is only characterized in the forward direction on normalizer representatives; consumers cannot rewrite an ordinary quotient representative through `.symm` without unfolding `MulEquiv.ofBijective`.

findings.evidence: Line 55 introduces `normalizerQuotientMk`, line 69 proves only `normalizerQuotientMk_eq_one_iff`; `rg -n "normalizerQuotientMk.*ker|ker.*normalizerQuotientMk"` finds no kernel lemma.
findings.fix: Add a characteristic lemma for the homomorphism kernel, e.g. `(normalizerQuotientMk H).ker = H.subgroupOf (Subgroup.normalizer (H : Set G))`, preferably `[simp]` if it normalizes well.
findings.issue: The quotient map lacks a kernel lemma, so the advertised kernel API is only available as the pointwise identity criterion at line 69.

summary: The named quotient is useful, but the public surface is relying on exposed bodies and is missing characteristic lemmas needed to use the normal-case comparison without unfolding implementation definitions.
